name: React Interview Foundations — additive questions
description: Question expansion derived from the attached source files; reuses existing repository topic IDs and defines only missing concepts.

topics.name: React Effects
topics.description: Synchronizing React components with external systems using effects and cleanup.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Synchronizing a component with external systems using useEffect and useLayoutEffect.

An effect is synchronization after rendering, not a general replacement for derived values or event handlers.

## What problem does it solve?

Incorrect dependencies cause stale closures, repeated work, races, subscriptions that leak, or loops.

## How it works

A search effect cancels or ignores an obsolete request when the query changes before the response returns.

- React runs an effect after commit when its dependencies changed
- Cleanup runs before the next relevant effect and on unmount
- useLayoutEffect runs before paint for layout-sensitive measurement but can block visual updates

For the interview question "Effects, dependencies, and cleanup", a complete answer should cover three things: Connects effects to external systems rather than arbitrary logic. Explains honest dependencies and cleanup before rerun or unmount. Uses it only for pre-paint layout-sensitive work.

## Common mistakes

- Suppressing dependency warnings to force a desired schedule
- Using an effect to calculate data that can be derived during render

## Key terms

- effect — Post-render synchronization with an external system
- dependency array — Values whose changes require resynchronization
- cleanup — Undoing a subscription or obsolete operation

questions.title: Applying React Render Snapshots and State Updates in practice
questions.prompt: Several counter increments should use functional updates when each new value depends on the previous one. Would you keep this approach? Explain the trade-offs, likely failure modes, and how you would verify it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Explains render snapshots
expectedPoints.description: States that current render values do not change in place.

expectedPoints.label: Explains batching
expectedPoints.description: Mentions queued updates and fewer renders.

expectedPoints.label: Uses functional updates correctly
expectedPoints.description: Connects them to previous-state-dependent calculations.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about React Render Snapshots and State Updates
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Expecting a setter to change the current local variable immediately." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Explains render snapshots
expectedPoints.description: States that current render values do not change in place.

expectedPoints.label: Explains batching
expectedPoints.description: Mentions queued updates and fewer renders.

expectedPoints.label: Uses functional updates correctly
expectedPoints.description: Connects them to previous-state-dependent calculations.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Mutating objects or arrays and passing the same reference back?

questions.title: Effects, dependencies, and cleanup
questions.prompt: What is useEffect for, how should dependencies and cleanup be handled, and when is useLayoutEffect justified?
questions.answerStructureHint: Start with synchronization, then cover lifecycle, stale work, and paint timing.

expectedPoints.description: Connects effects to external systems rather than arbitrary logic.

expectedPoints.label: Distinguishes layout effect
expectedPoints.description: Uses it only for pre-paint layout-sensitive work.

followUps.prompt: How would you prevent an older request from overwriting newer search results?

questions.title: Applying Controlled and uncontrolled form inputs in practice

questions.title: Applying Keys and reconciliation in practice
questions.prompt: A todo list should use each todo's database ID, not its current array position. Would you keep this approach? Explain the trade-offs, likely failure modes, and how you would verify it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Explains identity
expectedPoints.description: States that keys identify siblings across renders.

expectedPoints.label: Connects keys to state
expectedPoints.description: Explains preservation or reset of component and DOM state.

expectedPoints.label: Explains index risk
expectedPoints.description: Uses insertion, deletion, filtering, or reordering as the failure case.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Applying Context and prop drilling in practice
questions.prompt: Theme, locale, or authenticated user identity often fit context; temporary local form state usually does not. Would you keep this approach? Explain the trade-offs, likely failure modes, and how you would verify it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Defines the problem
expectedPoints.description: Connects context to subtree-wide dependency delivery and prop drilling.

expectedPoints.label: Explains update cost
expectedPoints.description: Notes consumer rerenders when the provider value changes.

expectedPoints.label: Sets appropriate boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Gives a cross-cutting use case and rejects one giant context.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Applying React memoization and render optimization in practice
questions.prompt: A slow filtered list may use useMemo after profiling, while a cheap arithmetic expression should stay direct. Would you keep this approach? Explain the trade-offs, likely failure modes, and how you would verify it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Distinguishes the tools
expectedPoints.description: Separates render skipping, value caching, and function identity caching.

expectedPoints.label: Explains reference stability
expectedPoints.description: Notes shallow comparison and invalidation by new references.

expectedPoints.label: Uses measurement
expectedPoints.description: Requires profiling and recognizes memoization overhead.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

expectedPoints.label: Distinguishes the tools

expectedPoints.label: Explains reference stability

expectedPoints.label: Uses measurement

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Wrapping every function in useCallback without a memoized consumer or dependency need?

questions.title: Applying useState, useReducer, and Redux Toolkit in practice
questions.prompt: A local toggle fits useState; a multi-step form with coordinated actions may fit useReducer; shared normalized server-independent state may justify Redux Toolkit. Would you keep this approach? Explain the trade-offs, likely failure modes, and how you would verify it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Uses narrow ownership
expectedPoints.description: Keeps state local unless broader sharing is justified.

expectedPoints.label: Chooses reducers by transition complexity
expectedPoints.description: Connects useReducer to coordinated actions and invariants.

expectedPoints.label: Justifies global tooling
expectedPoints.description: Mentions shared ownership, selectors, middleware, devtools, or scale.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Applying React Transitions and Update Priority in practice
questions.prompt: Separate the urgent state from the non-urgent result it triggers. Would you keep this approach? Explain the trade-offs, likely failure modes, and how you would verify it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Explains update priority
expectedPoints.description: Distinguishes urgent and non-urgent updates.

expectedPoints.label: Sets correct expectations
expectedPoints.description: States that a transition does not make computation or networking faster.

expectedPoints.label: Handles pending and stale UI
expectedPoints.description: Uses pending feedback or deferred rendering deliberately so older content is not presented as final without context.

questions.title: Applying Optimistic UI Reconciliation in practice
questions.prompt: Model the optimistic change and its lifecycle explicitly. Would you keep this approach? Explain the trade-offs, likely failure modes, and how you would verify it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Models temporary optimistic state
expectedPoints.description: Applies a reversible local result and makes pending uncertainty visible when it matters.

expectedPoints.label: Reconciles by request identity
expectedPoints.description: Matches each response to the correct pending operation and replaces local predictions with authoritative data.

expectedPoints.label: Handles failure and conflict
expectedPoints.description: Rolls back or exposes a recoverable error without discarding unrelated newer changes.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about Optimistic UI Reconciliation

questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Models temporary optimistic state
expectedPoints.description: Applies a reversible local result and makes pending uncertainty visible when it matters.

expectedPoints.label: Reconciles by request identity

expectedPoints.label: Handles failure and conflict

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Using array position instead of request identity when several operations overlap?
